nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; found on coral reef slopes prone to moderate currents, typically near large coral heads. Adults tend to occupy depths greater than 20 m; juveniles have been found in shallow lagoons and protected bays.

diet.notes: Tends to accept meaty foods such as krill, shrimp, crab, and whole silversides. Has been reported to take frozen preparations over time. Small live fish may be taken as prey. Large water volume and robust filtration are generally advised given the species' heavy bioload.

summary: The Leaflip Grouper is a large Indo-Pacific predator that reaches around 35 cm and is generally considered suitable only for expert aquarists, primarily due to its predatory nature and a toxic skin secretion that can prove lethal to other tank inhabitants if the fish is severely stressed or dies.
careNotes:
The Leaflip Grouper is a large, ambush-oriented predator that has been reported to grow to approximately 35 cm in well-kept aquariums. Tanks of at least 1,500 litres are recommended to provide adequate territory and dilute the heavy bioload this species produces.

One of the most important considerations with this species is its toxic skin mucus, known as grammistin, which deters predators in the wild. In captivity this toxin has been known to be released in quantity when the fish is severely stressed or dying, and multiple reports indicate it can be lethal to all other inhabitants in the aquarium. Maintaining stable water conditions and providing a tight-fitting lid — to prevent escape and reduce stress — are strongly recommended for this reason.

Juveniles tend to be more skittish; adults generally become tamer over time. The species is best kept singly in a predator tank with large, robust tankmates of similar size. Small fish, shrimp, and crabs are typically treated as prey.

Like other members of the soapfish family (Grammistidae), the Leaflip Grouper is protogynous, meaning individuals first mature as females and the dominant animal may later transition to male.

The Leaflip Grouper is also widely traded under the names Clown Grouper, Spotted Soapfish, Snowflake Soapfish, and Leaflip Soapfish.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Product page for Leaflip Grouper. Lists 125 gal minimum tank size, 72-78°F, SG 1.020-1.025, pH 8.1-8.4, Expert Only care level. Care level and water parameters used as primary reference.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Scientific data: max size 35.0 cm TL, wild temperature range 21.2-29°C, family Grammistidae, order Perciformes. Native range confirmed: Comoros to Line/Marquesan/Society Islands, north to Japan, south to New Caledonia, and South Africa. Used for taxonomy, size, and temperature verification.
